content:
# 1 Timothy Chapter 6 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/1TI_006_page_001.jpg, 1TI_006_page_002.jpg

1. Let as many as are slaves under [the] yoke consider their own masters worthy of all honor, that the name of God and the teaching may not be blasphemed.

2. And the [ones] having believing masters, let them not despise [them] because they are brothers, but rather let them serve, because the [ones] receiving the benefit of the good service are faithful and beloved. These things teach and urge.

3. If anyone teaches other doctrine and does not come to the sound words of our Lord Yeshua Christos and to the teaching according to godliness,

4. he has been puffed up, understanding nothing, but having a sickness for controversies and word-battles, from which come envy, strife, blasphemies, evil suspicions,

5. constant frictions of men corrupted in the mind and deprived of the truth, supposing the godliness to be a means of gain.

6. But godliness with contentment is great gain.

7. For we brought nothing into the world, and it is evident that neither can we carry anything out.

8. But having food and coverings, with these we will be content.

9. But the [ones] wanting to be rich fall into temptation and a snare and many foolish and harmful desires, which plunge the men into ruin and destruction.

10. For a root of all the evils is the love of money, which some, reaching after, have been led astray from the faith and have pierced themselves with many pains.

11. But you, O man of God, flee these things. But pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, endurance, gentleness.

12. Fight the good fight of the faith. Take hold of the eternal life, unto which you were called and you confessed the good confession before many witnesses.

13. I command you before the God, the [one] giving life to all things, and Christos Yeshua, the [one] having testified before Pontius Pilate the good confession,

14. [that] you keep the commandment spotless [and] without reproach until the appearing of our Lord Yeshua Christos,

15. which at [its] own times will show the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of the [ones] reigning and Lord of the [ones] exercising lordship,

16. the only [one] having immortality, dwelling in unapproachable light, whom no one of men has seen nor is able to see, to whom [be] honor and eternal might. Amen.

17. To the rich in the present age, command not to be haughty, nor to have hoped in [the] uncertainty of riches, but in God, the [one] providing us all things richly for enjoyment;

18. to do good, to be rich in good works, to be generous, willing to share,

19. storing up for themselves a good foundation for the future, that they may take hold of the [life] truly [being] life.

20. O Timothy, guard the deposit, turning away from the profane empty utterances and contradictions of the falsely named knowledge,

21. which some, professing, have missed the mark concerning the faith. The grace [be] with you. Amen.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.10 — "a root of all the evils":** rhiza pantōn tōn kakōn — Paul says "a root" (anarthrous — not "the root") of all evils is the love of money. The love of money is one root, not the only root.

2. **v.13 — "the good confession before Pontius Pilate":** The mention of Pilate grounds the confession in historical specificity — an early creedal element that would later appear in the Apostles' Creed.

3. **v.15-16 — The doxology:** Six divine attributes accumulating: blessed, only Sovereign, King of kings, Lord of lords, alone having immortality, dwelling in unapproachable light. The language is simultaneously Jewish and Hellenistic.

4. **v.20 — "the falsely named knowledge":** tēs pseudōnymou gnōseōs — "the falsely-called gnōsis." This may be an early reference to proto-Gnostic teaching. The term gnōsis ("knowledge") was later claimed by Gnostic movements.

— EMET, AI Translator
